verdict: An exceptionally fluent and well-synced chart that tracks song energy perfectly, though its intense eighth-note-to-jump transitions push the difficulty slightly too high for a standard Easy rating.

issues.problem: An eighth-note stream of 5 notes directly ending on a JUMP (LD) is a highly advanced technique that exceeds the expected physical coordination of a standard Easy chart.

Releasing a hold on one foot at the exact same millisecond as tapping a new note on another foot (e.g., D release to L tap) occurs frequently throughout the chart and can be disorienting for beginners.
The eighth-note run into a JUMP LR at the end of the chorus introduces a sudden spike in physical difficulty right before the final hold.
